name: Tide (Rust Async Web Framework)

performance.notes: async-std based; good but slower than hyper-based frameworks

agent_readiness.notes: Ergonomic async-std based Rust web framework. use tide; #[async_std::main] async fn main() { let mut app = tide::new(); app.at('/').get(|_| async { Ok('Hello, World!') }); app.listen('127.0.0.1:8080').await.unwrap() }. State: tide::with_state(state). JSON: req.body_json::<T>().await?; resp.body_json(&data)?. Route params: req.param('name')?. Middleware: app.with(middleware). Sessions: tide-sessions crate. async-std runtime (not tokio) — interop with tokio requires glue. Development slowed significantly post-2021. Last major release 0.16.0 in 2021.

gotchas: async-std runtime (not tokio) — limits ecosystem compatibility
No active maintenance for security patches
Axum and Rocket have overtaken in ergonomics
avoid_when: New projects — use Axum or Rocket instead
Production systems needing ongoing security support
summary: Tide is an ergonomic async-std Rust web framework. tide::new(); app.at('/').get(handler); app.listen(addr). State, JSON body_json(), route params. MIT/Apache-2.0 by http-rs. Development stalled since 2021 — no longer maintained. async-std runtime limits tokio ecosystem compatibility. Avoid for new projects — use Axum or Rocket. Legacy tide codebases only.
